original: You pull the injector out but remember that you need to have scales in order use it!\n\n
e.capitalA+e.short+" uses the scales of color to no effect.

A quick look at the injector reveals the tap-to-link emblem. Grabbing your codex and tapping the injector to it, the typical permission-to-link pop up [silly|pops up|appears]. A quick tap later and the interface appears. \n\n\t“Scales of color can only do one of the following per dose: change color of scales/chitin, alter, remove, or create body patterns/markings.” is printed in bold at the top of the program. There are two buttons below it, one for changing your scale/chitin color[pc.hasPartScales any||[pc.hasPartChitin any|| (which you won’t be using)]], and one for altering your body patterns/markings.

You watch in amazement as you[pc.hasAccentMarkings|r [pc.accentMarkings] ".concat(n.singularMarking(a)?"are":"is"," replaced by| gain]").concat(i," ").concat(o," ").concat(s,".\n\nA quick once-over confirms you now have").concat(i," ").concat(o," ").concat(s,".")

You adjust the selector to recolor your body markings to "+o+" and press the medipen to your sternum. There’s a quiet hiss from the device, followed by a tingling sensation that spreads from the injection site over your whole body. When the tingling finally ends you pull out your codex to check your reflection, finding that the [pc.skinAccent] of your [pc.accentMarkingsNoun] has changed to "+o+".

You adjust the selector to remove your body markings and press the medipen to your sternum. There’s a quiet hiss from the device, followed by a tingling sensation that spreads from the injection site over your whole body. When the tingling finally ends you pull out your codex to check your reflection, finding that your body no longer has [pc.accentMarkings].
